title: A photo on my phone I never delete
summary: Describe one photo on your phone and the day it was taken.
topic: A photo on my phone I never delete
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about one photo you never delete from your phone. Describe what is in the photo and explain the day it was taken. Use past simple and past continuous. Say why this photo is important to you.
hintStarters: There is one photo on my phone that I always keep…
I took this photo on the day when…
Every time I look at it, I feel…
sampleText: There is one photo on my phone that I never delete. It shows my grandmother sitting at her kitchen table in Guadalajara, holding a cup of coffee with both hands. The sun was coming through the window behind her, and the light made her face look warm and soft. I took this photo two years ago on a Sunday morning. We were talking about her childhood when I suddenly picked up my phone and pressed the button. She did not know I was taking the photo. She was looking down at her cup and smiling at something I said. In the background, you can see a yellow wall and some old plates. The photo is a little blurry because I took it quickly, but I do not care. Every time I scroll through my camera roll and see it, I feel the same thing I felt in that kitchen. My grandmother passed away last winter, so I cannot take any more photos of her. That picture is the last one I have from that Sunday. I never delete it because it reminds me of a quiet, ordinary morning that I did not know was special at the time.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: holding a cup of coffee with both hands
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cầm tách cà phê bằng cả hai tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The sun was coming through the window
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ánh mặt trời đang chiếu qua cửa sổ
đột nhiên cầm điện thoại lên

sampleAnnotations.phrase: She was looking down at her cup
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Bà đang nhìn xuống tách của mình
lướt qua thư viện ảnh của tôi
buổi sáng yên tĩnh, bình thường

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for background scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous để miêu tả bối cảnh đang diễn ra trong ảnh (was coming, was looking), và past simple cho hành động cụ thể (took, pressed).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for what was happening in the background ('the sun was coming', 'she was looking') and past simple for the specific action you did ('I took', 'I pressed').

sampleTranslationVi: Có một tấm ảnh trên điện thoại tôi mà tôi không bao giờ xóa. Ảnh chụp bà tôi đang ngồi ở bàn bếp ở Guadalajara, cầm tách cà phê bằng cả hai tay. Ánh mặt trời đang chiếu qua cửa sổ phía sau bà, ánh sáng làm khuôn mặt bà trông ấm áp và dịu dàng. Tôi chụp bức ảnh này hai năm trước vào một buổi sáng Chủ nhật. Chúng tôi đang nói chuyện về tuổi thơ của bà khi tôi đột nhiên cầm điện thoại lên và nhấn nút. Bà không biết tôi đang chụp ảnh. Bà đang nhìn xuống tách của mình và mỉm cười vì điều gì đó tôi nói. Ở phía sau, bạn có thể thấy một bức tường vàng và một vài chiếc đĩa cũ. Ảnh hơi mờ vì tôi chụp vội, nhưng tôi không quan tâm. Mỗi lần lướt qua thư viện ảnh và nhìn thấy nó, tôi cảm thấy y như lúc ở trong căn bếp đó. Bà tôi đã qua đời mùa đông năm ngoái, nên tôi không thể chụp thêm ảnh nào của bà nữa. Bức ảnh đó là bức cuối cùng tôi có từ ngày Chủ nhật ấy. Tôi không bao giờ xóa nó vì nó nhắc tôi nhớ đến một buổi sáng yên tĩnh, bình thường mà lúc đó tôi không biết là đặc biệt.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best opening sentence for this topic.
options: There is one photo on my phone that I never deleted.
There is one photo on my phone that I never delete.
There was one photo on my phone that I never delete.
There are one photo on my phone that I never delete.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'is' describes the photo that still exists now. 'Never delete' is a habit.
mcQuestions.hint: The photo still exists today — which tense fits?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past continuous for a background action?
options: The sun came through the window behind her.
The sun is coming through the window behind her.
The sun was coming through the window behind her.
The sun has come through the window behind her.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was coming') sets the background scene in a past memory.
mcQuestions.hint: Background descriptions in past stories use past continuous.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural collocation for taking a quick, unplanned photo.
options: I suddenly pick up my phone and press the button.
I suddenly picked up my phone and pressing the button.
I sudden picked up my phone and pressed the button.
I suddenly picked up my phone and pressed the button.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple for the sequence of actions: picked up, pressed.
mcQuestions.hint: Two past simple verbs in sequence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase best describes something seen behind the main subject?
options: In the background, you can see a yellow wall.
In the foreground, you can see a yellow wall.
Behind the camera, you can see a yellow wall.
On the top, you can see a yellow wall.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In the background' means the part of the image behind the main subject.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means 'behind the main subject in the photo'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'The photo is a little ___ because I took it quickly.'
options: blur
blurry
blurred badly
blurring
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Blurry' is an adjective meaning not clear or sharp.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word is an adjective meaning 'not clear'?

options: It reminds me about a quiet morning.
It remind me of a quiet morning.
It reminds me of a quiet morning.
It is remind me of a quiet morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Remind someone of something' is the correct collocation.
mcQuestions.hint: The verb pattern is 'remind + person + of + thing'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option expresses that someone died, using a polite phrase?
options: My grandmother was died last winter.
My grandmother has past away last winter.
My grandmother passed away in last winter.
My grandmother passed away last winter.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Passed away' is a polite expression for dying. Past simple is correct with 'last winter'.
mcQuestions.hint: No preposition before 'last winter'. 'Passed away' is past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence structure: 'Every time I ___ my camera roll and see it, I feel the same.'
options: scroll through
scroll across
scroll by
scroll up
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Scroll through' means to move through content on a screen.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to move through photos on a phone?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural way to say a photo was taken without the subject knowing.
options: She did not know I took the photo at the moment.
She did not know I was taking the photo.
She was not knowing I was taking the photo.
She does not know I was taking the photo.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Did not know' (past simple) + 'was taking' (past continuous) is the correct combination.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple for what she didn't know; past continuous for the action in progress.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means the image is not perfectly sharp but you don't mind?
options: a little blurry, so I do not care
a little blur, but I do not care
a little blurry, but I do not care
a little blurry, although I care
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows contrast between the imperfect quality and the speaker's lack of concern.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'because' to explain why you keep the photo?
options: I never delete it so it reminds me of that morning.
I never delete it although it reminds me of that morning.
I never delete it but it reminds me of that morning.
I never delete it because it reminds me of that morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' introduces the reason for keeping the photo.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a photo you have kept for a long time? What does it mean to you, and why do you think we keep some photos but delete others?
